mappings.rationaleAI: Covers the molecular level of biotic organization (A.2.a) including polymers, carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids, and addresses metabolism (A.1.b).
Directly covers the cellular level of biotic organization (A.2.b) including procaryotic and eucaryotic cells, nucleus, and endoplasmic reticulum.
Addresses metabolism (A.1.b) including catabolism and anabolism at the molecular level, and biochemical reactions within cells.
Covers nucleic acids and the molecular machinery of reproduction, growth, and development (A.1.d) at the molecular level of organization (A.2.a).
Directly addresses population-level biotic organization (A.2.d) including species, communities, and interaction with the environment (A.1.e).
Covers reproduction, growth, and development (A.1.d) including cell differentiation and the progression from blastula to organism (A.2.c).
Directly relevant to metabolism (A.1.b), covering the chemical reactions and proteins that drive catabolism and anabolism in living organisms.
Covers sensory reception (A.1.c) and the nervous system, addressing how organisms process stimuli and interact with their environment (A.1.e).
Addresses the molecular level of organization (A.2.a) through DNA, genes, and genomes, and reproduction at the cellular level (A.1.d and A.2.b).
Directly covers the concept of extraterrestrial life and its chemistry (B.1) and the significance of the search for life beyond Earth (B.2).
Relevant to the search for life beyond Earth (B.2), covering planetary environments where life might exist.
Illustrates interaction with the environment (A.1.e) and metabolism (A.1.b), showing how organisms respond to environmental cycles.
Covers procaryotic cells (A.2.b), microbial metabolism, reproduction, and the population/community levels of organization (A.2.d).
Addresses the integration of multiple levels of biotic organization (A.2) from molecular through organ systems.
